channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Comparing Trump and Bush: Continuities in US Right-Wing Approaches to War
item.description: The essay from The New York Times explores the similarities between Donald Trump’s approach to a potential conflict with Iran and George W. Bush’s invasion of Iraq. Drawing on a 2023 National Review p...

item.title: Johannesburg Initiates Tender to Appoint Managing Agent for Johannesburg Transport Interchange Centre
item.description: The City of Johannesburg has taken steps to finally get the Johannesburg Transport Interchange (JITI) Centre up and running after years of delays. Completed in 2021 at a cost of R516 million, the faci...

item.title: Kuki Students Body Raises Concerns Over Congress MPs' Remarks on Land Issue in Manipur
item.description: The Top Kuki Students' Body (KSO) has reportedly raised questions regarding certain statements made by Congress Members of Parliament from Manipur about land-related issues in the state. The KSO, repr...

item.title: US Embassy Issues Safety Alert for Its Facilities and Schools in Nigeria
item.description: Di US Embassy for Abuja don yan say dem don get info about possible wahala wey fit affect US facilities and schools for Nigeria. Di embassy dey advise all Americans wey dey Nigeria make dem dey watch ...

item.title: Claresholm to receive mobile mammography service for breast cancer screening
item.description: Claresholm, Alberta, will soon benefit from a mobile mammography service aimed at improving access to breast cancer screening in rural communities. The service, operated by healthcare professionals, w...

item.title: Federal University Dutse Takes Disciplinary Action Against 35 Students for Exam Malpractice
item.description: The Senate of Federal University Dutse for Jigawa don approve serious disciplinary action against some students wey involve themselves in examination malpractice. According to Abdullahi Yahaya-Bello, ...

item.title: Woman questions original identification after wrongful conviction in 2003 rape case
item.description: At Manchester Crown Court, a woman who was raped in Salford in 2003 testified that she initially identified Andy Malkinson in a police video identification procedure, leading to his wrongful convictio...

item.title: World Bank Approves Credit Guarantee Vehicle to Boost SA Infrastructure and Jobs
item.description: The World Bank has approved the South Africa Blended Finance Platform for Resilient Infrastructure Programme, marking a major step in accelerating infrastructure development and economic growth. The i...

item.title: Rep. Dan Crenshaw Defeated by Steve Toth in Texas GOP Primary
item.description: Republican Representative Dan Crenshaw, a four-term congressman from Texas's 2nd Congressional District, lost his Republican primary election on March 3, 2026, to state Representative Steve Toth. Toth...

item.title: Coco Martin and Julia Montes Perform Romantic Duet of 'Palagi' on ASAP to Promote Upcoming Series Sigabo
item.description: On the popular Sunday variety show ASAP, actors Coco Martin and Julia Montes, fondly called CocoJul by their fans, delighted audiences with a heartfelt duet of the hit song 'Palagi' by TJ Monterde and...

item.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley Switches to Independent Status Amid California Redistricting and Reelection Bid
item.description: Rep. Kevin Kiley, a Republican from California, has announced he is leaving the GOP effective immediately and registering as having no party preference, making him the sole Independent in the U.S. Hou...

item.title: Sky Brown wins second skateboarding world championship in Brazil
item.description: British teenager Sky Brown has claimed the skateboarding world championship title once more, securing victory at the competition in São Paulo, Brazil. At 17 years old, Brown celebrated her win with he...

item.title: Police arrest woman after shots fired near Rihanna’s Beverly Hills home
item.description: Late on Sunday night, there was serious wahala at Rihanna’s mansion in Beverly Hills, Los Angeles when a woman allegedly fired several shots towards her house. Police responded quickly after reports o...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei appointed as Iran's new Supreme Leader following father's death
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the 56-year-old son of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has been appointed as Iran's new Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts. This decision was announced shortly after midnight...

item.title: Understanding Why Some Mental Health Conditions Receive Less Empathy
item.description: Certain mental health conditions, like anxiety, depression, and ADHD, have gained wider acceptance in society. People are increasingly able to talk about these conditions at workplaces, homes, and onl...

item.title: Murder Suspect from 2021 Dutch Tycoon Case Rearrested for Sh400,000 Robbery in Mombasa
item.description: Police in Mombasa have re-arrested a suspect linked to the 2021 murder of Dutch businessman Herman Rouwenhorst after he was nabbed in a Sh400,000 robbery. The man, previously implicated in the killing...

item.title: G7 Finance Ministers Discuss Energy Supply Amid Rising Oil Prices
item.description: OTTAWA — The G7 finance ministers convened on Monday to discuss potential measures to support global oil supply as prices surged due to escalating tensions in Iran. While no immediate release of emerg...

item.title: World Dog Surf Champion Sugar Battles Cancer at Age 16
item.description: Sugar, the 16-year-old rescue therapy dog and five-time World Dog Surf Champion, is now facing a battle with cancer. Sugar’s owner, Ryan Rustan of Huntington Beach, shared the news on a GoFundMe page,...

item.title: Philippine Statistics Authority Recommends Cancellation of Mining Executive’s Birth Certificate
item.description: The Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) has recommended the cancellation of the birth certificate of mining executive Joseph Sy after a three-month delay that drew criticism from lawmakers. During a...

item.title: Christopher de Leon Reflects on Portraying Ramon Montenegro in “FPJ’s Batang Quiapo”
item.description: Veteran actor Christopher de Leon shares his experience portraying the character Ramon Montenegro in the hit television series “FPJ’s Batang Quiapo.” In a recent interview, de Leon discussed the depth...
